content: I'm trying to perfect my sourdough baking technique. The crust always comes out too hard.
Sourdough can be tricky! What hydration percentage is your dough at?
I've been using 75% hydration. I think I might need to adjust my baking temperature too. I just moved to Berlin last month and my oven here is different from what I had in Seattle.
Ah, European ovens can be quite different! What temperature are you baking at?
I've been doing 475°F for the first 20 minutes with steam, then lowering to 450°F. I'm Casey, by the way. I work as an environmental policy advisor for the EU Commission.
Nice to meet you, Casey! That temperature sounds reasonable. Have you tried the Dutch oven method? It really helps with crust development.
I have a Dutch oven but haven't tried it yet. I've been so busy exploring Berlin with Thor - he's my border collie. We've been hiking in the Grunewald forest almost every weekend.
That sounds wonderful! Thor must love all the new smells and sights. The Dutch oven method might be perfect for you since it creates its own steam environment.
I'll definitely give that a try this weekend. I've also been getting into drone photography to capture the city from above. Do you think I could photograph my bread from a drone angle?
Drone bread photography? Now that's creative! It would certainly give a unique perspective. Maybe capture it rising or baking through an oven window?
Haha, I hadn't thought of that! I could set up my GoPro inside the oven instead. Safety first with drones around food. Speaking of which, any tips for achieving that perfect open crumb structure?
For open crumb, focus on gentle handling during shaping and make sure your starter is very active. How often do you feed yours?
I feed it twice daily now that I'm on a regular work schedule at the Commission. Before the move, I was doing consulting work which had me traveling a lot.
Consistency is key! With your regular schedule now, you should see improvement. Would you like me to share my favorite sourdough recipe?
Yes please! I'm always looking to expand my baking repertoire. Maybe I'll bring some to the office next week - we have a policy meeting on Wednesday about urban green spaces.
Perfect! I'll send over the recipe. Your colleagues will be impressed with homemade sourdough at a policy meeting!
